source_paper_title: Specific Wasserstein Divergence Between Continuous Martingales
context:
Section 2.1, Remark 2.4 (page 8 in the parsed text; PDF page 8/31).

We consider the scaling limit of (2.1) for our choice \(\alpha_1(\cdot)=\pm W_1(\cdot, P)^p\) as an interesting open question which we do not tackle here. Our main result (Theorem 2.9) is a first step in that direction, and it pins down the right type of scaling needed.
title: Scaling limit of dynamic risk-measure duality with one-step penalty \(\pm W_1(\cdot,P)^p\) on path space
latex:
Given a time horizon \(1\) and an integer \(N\ge 1\), let \(X=(X_1,\dots,X_N)\) denote the coordinate process on \(\mathbb R^N\). Fix \(p>0\) and a reference one-step transition kernel \(P(\cdot\mid x)\in\mathcal P_1(\mathbb R)\) for each \(x\in\mathbb R\), and define the one-step penalty \(\alpha_1(q\mid x):=\pm W_1(q, P(\cdot\mid x))^p\) for any \(q\in\mathcal P_1(\mathbb R)\), where \(W_1\) is the \(1\)-Wasserstein distance on \(\mathbb R\).

For a probability law \(Q\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^N)\) such that the conditional laws \(Q(\mathrm d x_i\mid x_{1:i-1})\) are well-defined, define the time-consistent (additively decomposed) penalty
\[
\alpha_N(Q):=\mathbb E_Q\Big[\sum_{i=1}^N \alpha_1\big(Q(\cdot\mid X_{1:i-1})\mid X_{i-1}\big)\Big]
=\mathbb E_Q\Big[\sum_{i=1}^N \pm W_1\big(Q(\cdot\mid X_{1:i-1}),\,P(\cdot\mid X_{i-1})\big)^p\Big],
\]
(with the convention that \(X_0\) is deterministic or distributed according to a given initial law \(\mu\)). For a bounded measurable payoff \(F_N:\mathbb R^N\to\mathbb R\), define the corresponding convex risk functional
\[
\rho_N(F_N):=\sup_{Q\in\mathcal P(\mathbb R^N)}\Big\{\mathbb E_Q[F_N]-\alpha_N(Q)\Big\}.
\]
Let \(p_N:C([0,1];\mathbb R)\to\mathbb R^N\) be the projection on the grid \(\{1/N,\dots,N/N\}\) (or a continuous linear interpolation map), and let \(F\) be a bounded continuous functional on path space \(C([0,1];\mathbb R)\). Consider discretizations \(F_N:=N\,F\circ \tilde p_N\), where \(\tilde p_N\) is a chosen interpolation of \(\mathbb R^N\) into \(C([0,1];\mathbb R)\).

Problem: determine an appropriate scaling \(c_N>0\) (depending on \(p\)) and characterize the limit
\[
\lim_{N\to\infty} c_N\,\rho_N(F_N)
\]
(if it exists), including a variational representation of the limit as a continuous-time stochastic control/optimization problem over laws \(Q\in\mathcal P(C([0,1];\mathbb R))\) under which the canonical process is a martingale, and identifying the limiting penalty functional induced by the one-step penalty \(\alpha_1(q\mid x)=\pm W_1(q,P(\cdot\mid x))^p\).
location_in_paper: Section 2.1, Remark 2.4 (page 8 in the parsed text; PDF page 8/31).
